perturbation.original_text: Product. Shi Farms agrees to sell Product and Gridiron agrees to purchase 30,000 lbs. of hemp biomass ("Biomass") from Shi Farms. Biomass must contain a minimum of six percent (6%) total Cannabidiol (CBD/and or CBDA) and all Biomass must have less than three percent (3%) total TCH content. The Biomass must contain no contaminates that are above acceptable industry standards for processing Biomass including but not limited to: Mold and Mildew; Non- Hemp Plant Material; Soil; Insects; Rodent Droppings; Wet or Rotting Material; Heavy Metals; Residual Pesticides or Herbicides; Bacteria.
perturbation.changed_text: Product. Shi Farms agrees to sell Product and Gridiron agrees to purchase 30,000 lbs. of hemp biomass ("Biomass") from Shi Farms. Hemp Product must contain a minimum of six percent (6%) total Cannabidiol (CBD/and or CBDA) and all Hemp Product must have less than three percent (3%) total TCH content. The Biomass must contain no contaminates that are above acceptable industry standards for processing Biomass including but not limited to: Mold and Mildew; Non- Hemp Plant Material; Soil; Insects; Rodent Droppings; Wet or Rotting Material; Heavy Metals; Residual Pesticides or Herbicides; Bacteria.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Biomass' to 'Hemp Product' in the second sentence, we create ambiguity. The first sentence defines 'Biomass' as the agreed-upon product, but later, 'Hemp Product' is introduced without clarifying if it’s interchangeable with 'Biomass' or a distinct term. This in-text contradiction can lead to disputes about what the agreed-upon product specifications actually are.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Product. Shi Farms agrees to sell Product and Gridiron agrees to purchase 30,000 lbs. of hemp biomass ("Biomass") from Shi Farms. Hemp Product must contain a minimum of six percent (6%) total Cannabidiol (CBD/and or CBDA) and all Hemp Product must have less than three percent (3%) total TCH content. The Biomass must contain no contaminates that are above acceptable industry standards for processing Biomass including but not limited to: Mold and Mildew; Non- Hemp Plant Material; Soil; Insects; Rodent Droppings; Wet or Rotting Material; Heavy Metals; Residual Pesticides or Herbicides; Bacteria.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'Hemp Product' without defining it, while the rest of the contract relies on the term 'Biomass,' which is explicitly defined. This misalignment of terminology creates ambiguity and contradiction because it's unclear if 'Hemp Product' and 'Biomass' are interchangeable, leading to uncertainty about the agreed-upon specifications. This directly aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Both Parties agree that CBD potency numbers are determined by a third-party lab according to standard testing protocol which was provided by the seller. Buyer may take their own samples ("Product Samples") for testing as well.
perturbation.changed_text: Both Parties agree that CBD potency numbers are determined by a third-party lab according to standard testing protocol which was provided by Shi Farms. Buyer may take their own samples ("Goods Samples") for testing as well.
perturbation.explanation: The introduction of the term "Goods Samples" instead of "Product Samples" creates ambiguity. The original agreement defined 'Product', but 'Goods' is not defined. This lack of clarity might cause disputes about whether the 'Goods Samples' have the same standing as the 'Product Samples' when determining the quality or potency of the deliverable, especially regarding the certificate of analysis.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Delivery. Product Samples. The point of delivery of the Product Samples shall be a laboratory determined by Gridiron, if Gridiron determines that third party analysis of the Biomass is required for processing. Shi Farms shall be responsible to ship the Product Samples to the designated laboratory. Biomass. Shi Farms shall be responsible for delivery of the Biomass to the processor determined by Gridiron, in good form as described above "A. Product".
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Product Samples' to 'Goods Samples' introduces a contradiction. The contradicted text explicitly refers to 'Product Samples' and defines 'Product'. By using the undefined term 'Goods Samples,' the changed text creates ambiguity about whether the samples referred to in the Delivery section are the same as those the buyer is allowed to take. This creates uncertainty about Shi Farms' delivery obligations and the criteria for acceptable Biomass, constituting a strong in-text contradiction under the definition of Misaligned Terminology because the agreement is now using terms interchangeably without defining them, potentially leading to conflicting obligations. It is unclear whether 'Goods Samples' are the same as 'Product Samples', thus contradicting the section describing 'Product Samples'.

perturbation.original_text: Termination. Either Party may terminate this Agreement at any time prior to delivery of the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: Termination. Either Party may terminate this Agreement at any time prior to delivery of the Goods.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Product" to "Goods" introduces misaligned terminology. While "Product" is explicitly defined in Section 1.A as hemp biomass, "Goods" is not defined anywhere in the agreement. This ambiguity creates uncertainty regarding the termination clause, as it is now unclear whether the right to terminate extends until the delivery of the specific hemp biomass or any other unspecified 'Goods'. This contradicts the defined scope of the agreement, which primarily revolves around the sale and purchase of hemp biomass.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Product. Shi Farms agrees to sell Product and Gridiron agrees to purchase 30,000 lbs. of hemp biomass ("Biomass") from Shi Farms. Hemp Product must contain a minimum of six percent (6%) total Cannabidiol (CBD/and or CBDA) and all Hemp Product must have less than three percent (3%) total TCH content. The Biomass must contain no contaminates that are above acceptable industry standards for processing Biomass including but not limited to: Mold and Mildew; Non- Hemp Plant Material; Soil; Insects; Rodent Droppings; Wet or Rotting Material; Heavy Metals; Residual Pesticides or Herbicides; Bacteria.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Product' to 'Goods' introduces a contradiction because 'Product' is explicitly defined as hemp biomass, while 'Goods' is not defined. This creates ambiguity, as the termination clause now refers to an undefined term. The contradicted text specifies that Shi Farms agrees to sell 'Product' (hemp biomass), thus limiting the scope to a specific item. By using 'Goods', the termination clause could be interpreted to apply to anything, conflicting with the initial specific definition of 'Product' and creating uncertainty. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', since 'Goods' is used without being defined and thus creates conflicting obligations.
